High_Requirements: The DPU-BOOT CSC shall put the processor into diagnostic mode.
Low_Requirements: Standard I/O InterfaceThe write primitive does not block.  The buffer passed to write is written directly to the active portion of the ping pong buffer by the driver.  This primitive is valid only in TEST mode.  The write call will set errno to S_dci_MODE_ERROR and return ERROR if the driver is in NORMAL mode.  In TEST mode, the driver returns the number of bytes written.
Instruction:
Definition: In this task, explain the behavior and constraints of the write primitive in the Standard I/O Interface.
Emphasis & Caution: Valid only in TEST mode and non-blocking.
Things to Avoid: Do not claim it works in NORMAL mode.

High_Requirements: The DPU-CCM shall provide a mechanism for other CSCs to report errors for inclusion in the DPU_HK.
Low_Requirements: Error Collection and ReportingThe DPU-CCM CSC provides a centralized error reporting interface, ccmErrEnq(), that other FSW tasks use to report errors.  Each time it wakes, ccmTask() checks to see if it is time to form an error/event packet for transmission to the ground.  If so, ccmTask() calls ccmHkMkError() to actually create the packet and forward it to DPU-SCUI for transmission to the ground.
Instruction:
Definition: In this task, describe how errors are collected and reported by the CCM CSC.
Emphasis & Caution: Note periodic packet formation and forwarding path.
Things to Avoid: Do not invent alternative reporting paths.

High_Requirements: The DPU-RTOS shall install an hook routine which is invoked on system exception which shall attempt to record the interrupt vector to the LAST_BOOT_IVEC location in EEPROM prior to reboot.
Low_Requirements: Install Exception Handlers The RSC processor also generates an external interrupt for the Power Fail Interrupt.  The RSCVME Board Support Package of VxWorks? does not directly support access to this interrupt.  Some custom routines must be provided to access this interrupt. These functions are described below, and are contained in sysLibSup.c.
Instruction:
Definition: In this task, explain how custom routines install and handle the Power Fail external interrupt.
Emphasis & Caution: VxWorks BSP does not directly support this interrupt.
Things to Avoid: Do not assume native BSP support.

High_Requirements: The DPU-BIT shall perform MIL-STD-1553B internal BIT.
Low_Requirements: Built-In Test CSCThe Built-In Test (BIT) CSC is a Level 2 reuse component from the SSFF and INSTRUMENT Y projects.  The detailed design of the BIT CSC follows. The Built-In Tests CSC, identified DPU-BIT, performs the Stage 2 Built-In Tests (BIT).  The Stage 2 BIT includes a test of the SCM EDAC circuit, a checksum test on SCM PROM, a MIL-STD-1553B internal BIT, and a memory test of the DCI Data Buffers. The results of the BIT are recorded in the SYS_CNFG_AREA in EEPROM and are also maintained in DRAM.  If an error occurs in one of the functions performing the BIT, the test result will be reported as a failure in the test itself.
Instruction:
Definition: In this task, describe the scope, tests, and result handling of the Stage 2 Built-In Test CSC.
Emphasis & Caution: Results are stored in both EEPROM and DRAM.
Things to Avoid: Do not add unlisted tests.

High_Requirements: If commanded, during the collection of event data, the DPU-DPA shall produce PROD_ACS_MSG.
Low_Requirements: Command and Control CSCThe Command and Control (CCM) CSC is a Level 2 reuse component from the INSTRUMENT Y project.  The Command and Control CSC includes the following components:* A Control Task, ccmCtrlTask(), which initializes the DPU FSW and spawns other tasks at bootup, schedules the production of DPU housekeeping data packets, monitors the execution of other tasks, and schedules the execution of other periodic tasks such as the heartbeat message and the watchdog strobe; and* A Command Dispatch Task, ccmCmdTask(), which receives and dispatches real-time commands received from the SCU or the ICU.Major data structures include:* A Static Data table that keeps track of the operational state.  Data included in this table includes housekeeping production rates, CCM specific flags, and the number of commands executed.* A Command Queue into which commands are placed when they arrive, via interrupt, from the ICU or the SCU.* An Error/Event Queue which accumulates error and event codes which are reported by the DPU FSW.  These error and event codes are removed from the queue and placed into a telemetry packet at a given interval and included in DPU housekeeping data.DPUSDS5.12.1.1.1Flight Software InitializationThe Command and Control CSC is initialized by spawning the CCM Control Task, ccmCtrlTask(), from the operating system startup task, usrRoot().  After the task is spawned it calls a function, ccmInit(), which creates the error/event queue, instantiates needed semaphores and installs various ISRs.  Finally, it spawns the remaining tasks which comprise the DPU FSW. When the CCM Control Task starts up, it reads DPU configuration startup defaults from the SYSTEM_CONFIG_AREA in EEPROM.  If the SYSTEM_CONFIG_AREA checksum is bad, hard-coded defaults are used.  The CCM Control Task initializes the remaining CSCs by calling the applicable initialization function, or if the CSC has an associated task, by spawning the task using the VxWorks function, taskSpawn().  When the CCM Control Task initializes a CSC, it passes the startup defaults read from EEPROM as parameters to the task initialization function. In addition to its task initialization activities, ccmInit(), also initializes the command length verification table by calling ccmCmdLengthInit().  The command processor uses the created table to verify expected command lengths for newly received commands.
Instruction:
Definition: In this task, restate the Command and Control CSC structure and its flight software initialization sequence.
Emphasis & Caution: Mention command length verification table setup.
Things to Avoid: Do not alter task responsibilities.

High_Requirements: This DPU-TIS shall provide the capability for an application program to enable and disable automatic time synchronization.
Low_Requirements: Data Transfer to QueueDuring initialization, the TMALI CSC installs an ISR hook routine, tmaliTransferToQueueISR(),  into the DCI Driver which is invoked when:* the DCI interface reaches the programmed frame limit in the Ping-Pong buffer, or* the DCI interface detects a data receipt timeout.
Instruction:
Definition: In this task, explain when and why the TMALI transfer-to-queue ISR is invoked during initialization.
Emphasis & Caution: Triggered by frame limit or data timeout.
Things to Avoid: Do not misstate ISR trigger conditions.

High_Requirements: The DPU-SCUI shall communicate with the SCU according to the protocol defined in document 1143-EI-S19121.
Low_Requirements: Startup SequenceThe DPU FSW is booted using PROM-resident bootstrap software.  The bootstrap software performs a basic set of built-in tests, then copies the DPU FSW from EEPROM to DRAM and executes it.  In flight, there are two methods which can trigger the DPU FSW to boot:* power-on (cold boot), or* watchdog reset (warm boot, commanded reboot).
Instruction:
Definition: In this task, describe how the DPU flight software boots from PROM using EEPROM images.
Emphasis & Caution: Distinguish cold boot from warm boot.
Things to Avoid: Do not confuse boot triggers.

High_Requirements: The DPU-CCM shall collect DPU_HK and forward it to the DPU-SCUI.
Low_Requirements: Error Collection and ReportingThe DPU-CCM CSC provides a centralized error reporting interface, ccmErrEnq(), that other FSW tasks use to report errors.  Each time it wakes, ccmTask() checks to see if it is time to form an error/event packet for transmission to the ground.  If so, ccmTask() calls ccmHkMkError() to actually create the packet and forward it to DPU-SCUI for transmission to the ground.
Instruction:
Definition: In this task, describe how errors are reported, packaged, and sent to the ground by the CCM CSC.
Emphasis & Caution: Include the role of ccmTask() and ccmHkMkError().
Things to Avoid: Do not add alternative transmission paths.

High_Requirements: The DPU-RTOS shall provide a function to allow an application program to write to the Real-Time Clock registers on the RAD6000SC CPU Module.
Low_Requirements: Time SynchronizationThe time contained in the Clock Message is specified to be valid on the rising edge of the next 1PPS signal.  When the DPU receives the Clock Message, the 1553 ISR calls the tisTimeSync() function, forwarding the data portion of the clock message to the TIS CSC via the TIS_CLK_MSG data structure..  The tisTimeSync() function will immediately copy the spacecraft clock value to the DPU clock hardware registers. The DPU hardware is designed to latch in the new time on the rising edge of the next 1 PPS signal (the act of writing a new time to the hardware registers arms the latch function in the hardware).  Once the new time is latched, the hardware automatically disables the time latch function to avoid the potential of latching in an old time at the next 1 PPS signal.will be received within one second.  In the unlikely event a UT Delta is discarded, an event message is enqueued to ccmErrEnq() for informational purposes.
Instruction:
Definition: In this task, explain how the DPU synchronizes its clock using the Clock Message and 1PPS signal.
Emphasis & Caution: Time is latched on the next rising 1PPS edge.
Things to Avoid: Do not change the latch or ISR sequence.

High_Requirements: The DPU-EDAC shall record the number of SBEs that have been detected in PROM and EEPROM since boot and the address of the last two SBEs.
Low_Requirements: DRAM BITPart of the memory is tested in Boot Sequence 1, and part of the memory is tested during Boot Sequence 2.  During Boot Sequence 1, ? KB of DRAM is tested before the Bootstrap is copied to this area.  During Boot Sequence 2, the  remaining memory is tested in ? KB blocks. The DRAM BIT first writes the address of each word in a block to that word in the block.   Then the test flushes the cache and reads back the entire block.  If a single-bit error occurs on the read-back, the location is read again.  If another single-bit error occurs, the test assumes a hard error and marks the whole block bad.  If a multiple-bit error occurs, the whole block is marked bad.  If the value read back does not match the address, the whole block is marked bad.  This same process is repeated with the ones complement of the address. The Bootstrap stores the results of the DRAM BIT in EEPROM as a series of x consecutive y-bit words.  Each bit in a result word represents the result of testing a ? KB block of DRAM.  These bits are mapped to DRAM by proceeding from least-significant bit (LSb) to most-significant bit (MSb) as the addresses of the memory blocks increase.  A set bit indicates that a block of DRAM has failed.  A clear bit indicates that a block of DRAM has passed the test.
Instruction:
Definition: In this task, summarize how the DRAM Built-In Test is performed across boot sequences and how results are recorded.
Emphasis & Caution: Describe block failure rules and bit mapping.
Things to Avoid: Do not alter test criteria or result encoding.

High_Requirements: The DPU-DCI shall report errors to an application program by setting the ERRNO task variable and returning ERROR.
Low_Requirements: Public FunctionsThis routine is called by any CSC in order to report an error or event that should be included in DPU housekeeping.  If this routine is called from interrupt context a static global variable, ccmISRError, is set so that the error can be enqueued later (see ccmCtrlTask()).  This is done since the error/event queue is semaphore-protected and a semaphore cannot be taken in an ISR.  The error queue semaphore has priority inversion set to reduce conflicts between multiple callers should a priority inversion situation arise. This routine also replaces frequently occuring errors with a special repeat error code.  The repeat error code is a special error code that follows a normally reported error code to indicate that the normally reported error code previously reported has occurred more than once in the last high rate reporting period
Instruction:
Definition: In this task, describe how CSCs report errors/events and how ISR-reported errors are deferred.
Emphasis & Caution: Semaphores cannot be taken in ISR context.
Things to Avoid: Do not enqueue errors directly from an ISR.

High_Requirements: The DPU-TIS shall provide the capability for an application program to set the value of the SC_TIME register(s) on the SCM and enable the 1PPS clock jam register, if automatic time synchronization is enabled.
Low_Requirements: Public FunctionsThis routine is used to format messages for transmission to the ICU and to place those messages on the SSI Driver Transmitter Queue. The functionCode parameter is used to create the CCSDS Packet APID and the Message Identifier (refer to document DPUICD-01).  A mutual exclusion semaphore, icuiSemWrite, protects the SSI Driver from being written by multiple tasks simultaneously.  The timeout parameter lets the caller control how long it is willing to block on this semaphore if there is an access conflict.This routine may be called at interrupt context to transmit an ACK/NAK message.  In that case, the semaphore is not taken since it is not valid to take a semaphore at interrupt context.  Refer to discussion of the design to accommodate the ACK/NAK message.
Instruction:
Definition: In this task, explain how messages are formatted and queued for ICU transmission via the SSI driver.
Emphasis & Caution: Semaphore is not taken in interrupt context for ACK/NAK.
Things to Avoid: Do not require semaphore use in ISR context.

High_Requirements: The DPU-ICUI shall use DPU-SSI to communicate with the SSI Interface.
Low_Requirements: Receiving Commands from the ICUThe interface to the ICU is via a synchronous serial interface (SSI) bus, which utilizes an inter-block gap (IBG) to delineate complete messages from one another.  The DPU hardware produces an interrupt when it detects an IBG, and the SSI Driver provides a mechanism by which an application can install an interrupt hook routine to respond to an IBG interrupt.  Upon initialization, the ICUI CSC installs a hook routine into the SSI Driver called icuiCommandReceiveISR(), which verifies the checksum of an incoming command, transmits an ACK/NAK message to the ICU, and then enqueues the command for later processing by calling ccmCmdEnq().
Instruction:
Definition: In this task, describe how commands from the ICU are detected, validated, acknowledged, and queued.
Emphasis & Caution: Triggered by IBG interrupt and checksum verification.
Things to Avoid: Do not bypass ACK/NAK handling.

High_Requirements: The DPU-BOOT CSC shall attempt to complete its normal execution sequence following detection of any failure.
Low_Requirements: Error Detection and Correction CSCThe Error Detection and Correction (EDAC) CSC is a Level 1 reuse component from the SSFF and INSTRUMENT Y projects.  The EDAC CSC consists of a set of Interrupt Service Routines (ISRs) and a Memory Scrubber Task.  The design of the INSTRUMENT Y EDAC CSC is described in document CIDPSDS-01.The only design change was to add logic to check for excessive external (SCM) EDAC interrupts.
Instruction:
Definition: In this task, summarize the purpose and components of the EDAC CSC and its design change.
Emphasis & Caution: Note added logic for excessive external EDAC interrupts.
Things to Avoid: Do not add unmentioned EDAC features.

High_Requirements: The DPU-CCM shall process real-time non-deferred commands within B ms of receipt from the ICU or the SCU.
Low_Requirements: Structural DesignThe TIS CSC provides a library of functions to* initialize the DPU clock hardware at startup,* maintain synchronization of the DPU clock with the Spacecraft clock,* provide a function for use by other applications to retrieve the current values of the DPU clock and the UT Delta, and provide functions for use by the CCM command dispatcher to alter the configuration of the clock interface.
Instruction:
Definition: In this task, outline the functions provided by the TIS CSC for clock initialization, sync, access, and configuration.
Emphasis & Caution: Include UT Delta handling.
Things to Avoid: Do not introduce unrelated clock services.

High_Requirements: The DPU-BIT shall perform two write/read/compare tests on the RAM for the DCI Data Buffers.  The first test shall write the address of each location to that location.  The second test shall write the ones complement of the values used in the first test.
Low_Requirements: Telescope Module Access Library and Interface CSCThe TMALI CSC includes the following components:* A queue, TMALI_EVENT_QUEUE, implemented using a ring buffer, which contains the detector events which have been received over the data capture interface from the DCI Driver, but which have not yet been retrieved by the DPA CSC;* An API which facilitates the retrieval of detector events from the queue by the DPA CSC.  The API consists of the tmaliNextEvent() and tmaliWait() functions.* An set of API functions used by the DPA CSC to configure the DCI interface, including tmaliBitArrangementSet(), tmaliDciFrameDepthGet(), tmaliDciFrameDepthSet(), tmaliDciWindowSet(), tmaliDataTimeoutSet(), and tmaliDataTimeoutGet().* An API housekeeping function, tmaliHkGet(), which gathers  CSC housekeeping data at the request of the Command and Control Task.* The task tmaliTask() and an ISR tmaliTransferToQueueISR() work together via a semaphore to synchronize the transfer of data from the DCI to the  queueAn error handling ISR, tmaliDciErrorReportedISR(), is installed as a hook routine to the DCI Driver, and is used to keep track of the number of errors reported by the DCI driver.
Instruction:
Definition: In this task, describe the components, APIs, tasks, and ISRs of the TMALI CSC.
Emphasis & Caution: Include queue-based event handling and DCI synchronization.
Things to Avoid: Do not omit error handling ISR.

High_Requirements: The DPU-DPA shall perform bounds checking before accessing memory.
Low_Requirements: Event ProcessingIf the Mode command is Instrument Y Mode, the detector events are spatially filtered to the Instrument Y window specified in the Mode command then the corresponding pixel in the Ping or Pong buffer is incremented.  If specified in the Mode command, pixels will be binned at AxA or BxB.  Also, if specified in the Mode command, a tracking frame data product will be produced for every Ping/Pong tracking frame. Instrument Y and Event Mode may be commanded simultaneously in the Mode command. If the ICU sends an XRT Position command, the Instrument Y mode and Event Mode windows will be resized to match the new window sizes in the command.
Instruction:
Definition: In this task, explain how detector events are filtered, binned, and accumulated in Instrument Y and Event modes.
Emphasis & Caution: Window resizing follows XRT Position commands.
Things to Avoid: Do not ignore Ping/Pong buffer behavior.

High_Requirements: The DPU-BIT shall report BIT_HK on request.
Low_Requirements: Instrument Control Unit Interface CSCThe Instrument Control Unit Interface (ICUI) CSC is a new component that provides support for application-level communication with the INSTRUMENT X ICU.  The ICUI CSC is primarily responsible for implementing the application protocol with the ICU as defined in document DPUICD-01. In order to meet these objectives, ICUI provides: * An ICU Interface Message Send Function, icuiEnq(), which provides an API by which other CSCs can send messages to the ICU.* An ICU Interface Command Reader ISR, icuiCmdReceiveISR(), which receives Telecommands from the ICU and forwards them to the Command and Control Task for processing.* An ICU Interface Housekeeping Function, icuiHkGet(), which gathers ICU Interface housekeeping data at the request of the Command and Control Task.
Instruction:
Definition: In this task, describe the role and key functions of the ICUI CSC for communication with the Instrument X ICU.
Emphasis & Caution: Follow the protocol defined in DPUICD-01.
Things to Avoid: Do not add unsupported ICUI functions.

High_Requirements: The DPU-BOOT CSC shall provide a monitor which accepts commands over the RS-232 interface.
Low_Requirements: Built-In Test CSCThe Built-In Test (BIT) CSC is a Level 2 reuse component from the SSFF and INSTRUMENT Y projects.  The detailed design of the BIT CSC follows. The Built-In Tests CSC, identified DPU-BIT, performs the Stage 2 Built-In Tests (BIT).  The Stage 2 BIT includes a test of the SCM EDAC circuit, a checksum test on SCM PROM, a MIL-STD-1553B internal BIT, and a memory test of the DCI Data Buffers. The results of the BIT are recorded in the SYS_CNFG_AREA in EEPROM and are also maintained in DRAM.  If an error occurs in one of the functions performing the BIT, the test result will be reported as a failure in the test itself.
Instruction:
Definition: In this task, summarize the purpose, tests, and result handling of the Stage 2 Built-In Test CSC.
Emphasis & Caution: Results are stored in both EEPROM and DRAM.
Things to Avoid: Do not include tests not listed.

High_Requirements: The DPU-DCI shall provide an application program with the ability to toggle the hardware interface between Normal mode and Test mode.
Low_Requirements: Data Capture Interface Driver CSCThe Data Capture Interface (DCI) Driver CSC is a new driver which provides a standard VxWorks driver interface to the DCI hardware.  The driver features two modes: a test mode and a normal mode.
Instruction:
Definition: In this task, explain the purpose and operating modes of the DCI Driver CSC.
Emphasis & Caution: Distinguish test mode from normal mode.
Things to Avoid: Do not mix mode behaviors.

High_Requirements: The DPU-BOOT CSC shall include a CPU BIT consisting of a branch test, a fixed point test, a floating point test, an interrupt test, a timer test, and a register test.
Low_Requirements: Built-In Test CSCThe Built-In Test (BIT) CSC is a Level 2 reuse component from the SSFF and INSTRUMENT Y projects.  The detailed design of the BIT CSC follows. The Built-In Tests CSC, identified DPU-BIT, performs the Stage 2 Built-In Tests (BIT).  The Stage 2 BIT includes a test of the SCM EDAC circuit, a checksum test on SCM PROM, a MIL-STD-1553B internal BIT, and a memory test of the DCI Data Buffers. The results of the BIT are recorded in the SYS_CNFG_AREA in EEPROM and are also maintained in DRAM.  If an error occurs in one of the functions performing the BIT, the test result will be reported as a failure in the test itself.
Instruction:
Definition: In this task, restate the scope and behavior of the Stage 2 Built-In Test CSC.
Emphasis & Caution: Failures are reported as test failures.
Things to Avoid: Do not change failure criteria.

High_Requirements: The Bootstrap Monitor of the DPU-BOOT CSC shall provide the capability to load a configuration of the DPU-RTOS from EEPROM to DRAM and start its execution on command.
Low_Requirements: Hardware InterfacesThe DPU-BOOTSTRAP must interface directly with the EEPROM on the Company X Communication Module.  The DPU-BOOTSTRAP accesses EEPROM as ?-bit words only.  The specific timing requirements of the EEPROM are satisfied in one of three ways:* the timing requirements are met by inserting specific wait loops (typical),* the timing requirements are met by use of the EEPROM Driver (DOWNLOAD command), andthe timing requirements are met implicitly by the amount of time which will be required to execute the intervening instructions regardless of the CPU speed setting (DRAM test logic).
Instruction:
Definition: In this task, describe how the DPU-BOOTSTRAP accesses EEPROM and meets timing requirements.
Emphasis & Caution: EEPROM is accessed only as fixed-size words.
Things to Avoid: Do not invent additional timing methods.

High_Requirements: The DPU-ICUI shall report errors to DPU-CCM for inclusion in the DPU_HK.
Low_Requirements: Error Collection and ReportingThe DPU-CCM CSC provides a centralized error reporting interface, ccmErrEnq(), that other FSW tasks use to report errors.  Each time it wakes, ccmTask() checks to see if it is time to form an error/event packet for transmission to the ground.  If so, ccmTask() calls ccmHkMkError() to actually create the packet and forward it to DPU-SCUI for transmission to the ground.
Instruction:
Definition: In this task, explain how the CCM CSC collects errors and sends error/event packets to the ground.
Emphasis & Caution: Packet creation is time-checked on task wakeup.
Things to Avoid: Do not bypass ccmHkMkError().

High_Requirements: The DPU-BOOT CSC shall enable EDAC on the SCM.
Low_Requirements: InitializationThe DPU-BIT should be initialized every time the DPU boots.  The startup task, usrRoot() should call bitPart1() before initializing the device drivers and bitPart2() after initializing EDAC and the device drivers.
Instruction:
Definition: In this task, describe when and how the DPU-BIT initialization functions are called during boot.
Emphasis & Caution: Call bitPart1() before and bitPart2() after driver initialization.
Things to Avoid: Do not reverse the call order.

High_Requirements: The DPU-SCUI shall be capable of preparing one STPDU for delivery to the SCU every M milliseconds, or shall indicate a No Data condition to the SCU by maintaining an un-incremented ST_PDU Transfer Request Counter field.
Low_Requirements: Telemetry Output to the Spacecraft 1553 BusThe SCUI CSC reads the Data Packages from the queues and transforms them into CCSDS Version 1 Source Packets.  These Source Packets are then packed into the STPDU for transmission to the SCU.  This processing is performed by scuiConvertToSTPDU().   The STPDU, prefixed with a Transfer Request Counter (TRC), is then written to fifteen sequential 1553 subaddresses (as described in the Company X 1553 ICD) by the function scuiWrite().
Instruction:
Definition: In this task, summarize how telemetry data is converted and written to the spacecraft 1553 bus.
Emphasis & Caution: Data is written to fifteen sequential subaddresses.
Things to Avoid: Do not alter packet formatting steps.

High_Requirements: The DPU-TMALI shall install callbacks for handling all DPU-DCI interrupts including Error interrupt, Ping-Pong Timeout interrupt, and Ping-Pong Complete Flag interrupt.
Low_Requirements: Install Exception HandlersThis routine installs a hook routine for the Memory Error Interrupt.  The hook routine will be called from the interrupt context.  This function returns OK always.
Instruction:
Definition: In this task, explain how the memory error interrupt handler is installed and invoked.
Emphasis & Caution: The hook routine runs in interrupt context.
Things to Avoid: Do not assume task-level execution.

High_Requirements: The DPU-RTOS shall be a real-time, multi-tasking operating system.
Low_Requirements: Control and Monitoringthe CCM Control Task also handles memory dump commands.  In the event of a data dump command, the CCM Control Task will break the dump into manageable pieces and dump a small portion at a time, each time the task is awakened.  The purpose of this deferred activity is to prevent a large dump from consuming available CPU time by keeping the high-priority CCM Command Dispatch Task busy for an extended period.
Instruction:
Definition: In this task, describe how the CCM Control Task handles memory dump commands without blocking high-priority tasks.
Emphasis & Caution: Dumps are deferred and split into small pieces.
Things to Avoid: Do not process large dumps in a single wake cycle.

High_Requirements: The DPU-DPA shall be capable of providing DPA_HK on request.
Low_Requirements: Development PlatformThe DPU FSW will be implemented in the ANSI C programming language, with some PowerPC assembly used in the Bootstrap and to implement the VME data transfer in the DCI Driver.  The host (development) platform and operating system is Sun/Solaris from Sun Microsystems, Inc.  The target platform is the RAD6000 processor which has a POWER (PowerPC-like) architecture, and executes the VxWorks 5.3.1 real-time operating system.  The cross compiler and debugger is MULTI/C/C++ 1.8.9 from Green Hills Software, Inc.
Instruction:
Definition: In this task, summarize the development and target platforms, programming languages, operating systems, and toolchain used.
Emphasis & Caution: Clearly distinguish host vs. target platform.
Things to Avoid: Adding assumptions not stated in the text.

High_Requirements: The DPU-TMALI shall be capable of making data available from the DCI to DPU-DPA.  DPU-TMALI will populate a ring buffer with frames of data for application task retrieval.
Low_Requirements: Public FunctionsThis function returns the next EVENT in the queue to the caller.  The caller should store the value returned by this function in a variable defined locally within the scope of the function from which it is called.  If the return value is stored as described above, the EVENT value is stored in a RAD6000 register rather than memory.  Processing is therefore much faster since register accesses on the RAD6000 are much faster than memory accesses.A compilation option TMALI_NOCHECKS is provided for the disabling of buffer empty checks. This results in slightly faster code but should only be used when the application using this function is known to make proper calls to the function.  If the TMALI module is compiled with the TMALI_NOCHECKS option an improper call to the tmaliNextEvent function (calling it when no data is available in the TMALI buffer) will compromise the TMALI buffer data structure.The queue structure and the related pointers are described in the local tmaliP.h header file. As only the write pointer (pOut) is manipulated here and the operations on the 32 bit pointers themselves are 'atomic' no mutual exclusion problem can occur.
Instruction:
Definition: In this task, explain the purpose, usage, and performance considerations of the public function that returns the next EVENT from a queue.
Emphasis & Caution: Highlight the TMALI_NOCHECKS option and its risks.
Things to Avoid: Ignoring constraints about improper function calls.

High_Requirements: When commanded to event mode by the ICU,  the DPU-DPA shall be capable of collecting time-tagged photon events and producing a PROD_EVENT.
Low_Requirements: Bootstrap MonitorThe Bootstrap Monitor checks entered commands for syntax and number of arguments, and displays an error message to the RS-232 interface if an invalid command or argument is entered.  A complete listing of these messages is given in document 7384-BSPS-01.
Instruction:
Definition: In this task, describe the role of the Bootstrap Monitor in command validation and error reporting.
Emphasis & Caution: Mention where the complete error message list is documented.
Things to Avoid: Detailing commands not referenced in the text.

High_Requirements: The DPU-EDAC shall record the number of SBEs that have been detected in DRAM since boot and the address of the last two SBEs.
Low_Requirements: Real-Time Clock InterfaceThe Real-Time Clock (RTC) Registers of the RAD6000 are special-purpose registers not normally accessible to C language programs.  Some assembler wrapper functions provide access to these.
Instruction:
Definition: In this task, explain how C programs access the RAD6000 Real-Time Clock registers.
Emphasis & Caution: Note that access is only through assembler wrapper functions.
Things to Avoid: Claiming direct C-level register access.

High_Requirements: The DPU-BOOT CSC shall initialize Error Detection and Correction (EDAC) on the RSC by clearing DRAM.
Low_Requirements: InitializationThe DPU-BIT should be initialized every time the DPU boots.  The startup task, usrRoot() should call bitPart1() before initializing the device drivers and bitPart2() after initializing EDAC and the device drivers.
Instruction:
Definition: In this task, describe when and how the DPU-BIT initialization functions are called during boot.
Emphasis & Caution: Preserve the correct call order of bitPart1() and bitPart2().
Things to Avoid: Reordering or omitting initialization steps.

High_Requirements: The DPU-TMALI shall provide a function which sets the current event bit ordering mode in the DCI driver.
Low_Requirements: Event ProcessingIf the Mode command is Instrument Y Mode, the detector events are spatially filtered to the Instrument Y window specified in the Mode command then the corresponding pixel in the Ping or Pong buffer is incremented.  If specified in the Mode command, pixels will be binned at AxA or BxB.  Also, if specified in the Mode command, a tracking frame data product will be produced for every Ping/Pong tracking frame. Instrument Y and Event Mode may be commanded simultaneously in the Mode command. If the ICU sends an XRT Position command, the Instrument Y mode and Event Mode windows will be resized to match the new window sizes in the command.
Instruction:
Definition: In this task, summarize how detector events are processed under different Mode command configurations.
Emphasis & Caution: Cover filtering, binning, tracking frames, and window resizing conditions.
Things to Avoid: Treating Instrument Y Mode and Event Mode as mutually exclusive.

High_Requirements: The DPU-BOOT CSC shall test and clear DRAM on power-on using the COLD_MEM_SIZE obtained from the SYSTEM_BLOCK.
Low_Requirements: Instrument Control Unit Interface CSCThe Instrument Control Unit Interface (ICUI) CSC is a new component that provides support for application-level communication with the INSTRUMENT X ICU.  The ICUI CSC is primarily responsible for implementing the application protocol with the ICU as defined in document DPUICD-01. In order to meet these objectives, ICUI provides: * An ICU Interface Message Send Function, icuiEnq(), which provides an API by which other CSCs can send messages to the ICU.* An ICU Interface Command Reader ISR, icuiCmdReceiveISR(), which receives Telecommands from the ICU and forwards them to the Command and Control Task for processing.* An ICU Interface Housekeeping Function, icuiHkGet(), which gathers ICU Interface housekeeping data at the request of the Command and Control Task.
Instruction:
Definition: In this task, describe the responsibilities and provided APIs of the Instrument Control Unit Interface CSC.
Emphasis & Caution: Align functions with their specific roles in communication and housekeeping.
Things to Avoid: Mixing ICUI responsibilities with other CSCs.

High_Requirements: The DPU-ADC shall provide an application program with a function to perform an alternating read/setup-start sequence which will read the A/D result setup on the last call store the result into the scan list, and setup the conversion for the next item in the scan list; then on the next call start the conversion which was setup during the last call.
Low_Requirements: Telescope Module Access Library and Interface CSCThe TMALI CSC includes the following components:* A queue, TMALI_EVENT_QUEUE, implemented using a ring buffer, which contains the detector events which have been received over the data capture interface from the DCI Driver, but which have not yet been retrieved by the DPA CSC;* An API which facilitates the retrieval of detector events from the queue by the DPA CSC.  The API consists of the tmaliNextEvent() and tmaliWait() functions.* An set of API functions used by the DPA CSC to configure the DCI interface, including tmaliBitArrangementSet(), tmaliDciFrameDepthGet(), tmaliDciFrameDepthSet(), tmaliDciWindowSet(), tmaliDataTimeoutSet(), and tmaliDataTimeoutGet().* An API housekeeping function, tmaliHkGet(), which gathers  CSC housekeeping data at the request of the Command and Control Task.* The task tmaliTask() and an ISR tmaliTransferToQueueISR() work together via a semaphore to synchronize the transfer of data from the DCI to the  queueAn error handling ISR, tmaliDciErrorReportedISR(), is installed as a hook routine to the DCI Driver, and is used to keep track of the number of errors reported by the DCI driver.
Instruction:
Definition: In this task, outline the components, APIs, tasks, and ISRs that make up the TMALI CSC.
Emphasis & Caution: Emphasize queue handling, synchronization, and error tracking.
Things to Avoid: Omitting any major listed component or function.

High_Requirements: The DPU-SCUI shall time stamp the secondary header of the Source Packets with the current SC_TIME retrieved from the DPU-TIS module.
Low_Requirements: Control and MonitoringEvery time the CCM Control executes, it calls ccmPerProcess() to handle periodic processing responsibilities.  Such responsibilities include analog to digital conversion updates, DPU task monitoring, ICU heartbeat message production, and watchdog strobe. The ccmHealthChk() function, called by ccmPerProcess() verifies the execution of other tasks by monitoring the amount of time that has elapsed since each task last reported.  Other tasks report their execution to the CCM Control Task by calling the function, ccmTaskReport(), providing their task index.  Each task has an expected execution frequency, and if a task does not execute as expected, an error is reported in DPU housekeeping.  If the Command Dispatch Task fails to report for an extended period, the DPU will execute a reboot, since it is impossible to command the DPU if this task is not executing, otherwise it will strobe the watchdog
Instruction:
Definition: In this task, explain the periodic control, monitoring, and fault-handling responsibilities of the CCM Control.
Emphasis & Caution: Note conditions that trigger error reporting versus reboot.
Things to Avoid: Overlooking task reporting and expected execution frequency.

High_Requirements: The DPU-TMALI shall provide a function which sets the current event bit ordering mode in the DCI driver.
Low_Requirements: Public FunctionsThis routine provides a means to set the bit arrangement used by the DCI.  When bSpecialArrangement is TRUE, the DCI is set to special bit arrangement mode, otherwise the default mode of no rearrangement is set.
Instruction:
Definition: In this task, describe the function that sets the DCI bit arrangement and its effect based on the input flag.
Emphasis & Caution: Clearly state the behavior when bSpecialArrangement is TRUE or FALSE.
Things to Avoid: Introducing unsupported bit arrangement modes.

High_Requirements: The DPU-SCUI shall cancel the write to each subaddress if the SCU fails to read a pending STPDU after a timeout specified by a value calculated based on the SCU_POLL_RATE parameter provided during initialization, shall record new data to those subaddresses, and report an error to the DPU-CCM.  The SCU_POLL RATE parameter shall have a default setting of J ms and shall be capable of being modified dynamically.
Low_Requirements: Public FunctionsSets the poll rate and buffer rate to the given values.  If the values are out of range, the prior values are retained and an error is reported.  The scuiTask() semaphore timeout (scuiTaskTimeout) is automatically adjusted based on the given poll rate, by setting the timeout to approximately  the poll rate (not that the timeout is expressed in clock ticks, and so the resolution is low and only approximates  of the poll rate).
Instruction:
Definition: In this task, describe how the function sets poll rate and buffer rate and handles invalid values.
Emphasis & Caution: Note automatic timeout adjustment and low clock-tick resolution.
Things to Avoid: Ignoring error reporting on out-of-range values.

High_Requirements: The DPU-TIS shall provide the capability for an application program to select the active 1PPS signal.
Low_Requirements: CPU BITThe CPU BIT consists of a series of tests designed to test each of the functional units of the processor.  These include test for the Branch-Unit, the Fixed-Point Unit, the Floating-Point Unit, the Interrupt-Unit, and the Timer Unit.   Each of these tests consists of executing a few selected instructions from the this functional unit. The General Purpose Registers (GPR), the Segment Registers (SR), the Counter Register (CTR), and the Condition Register are tested for stuck-bits as part of the Branch-Unit test.  This is done by writing a 1s pattern (verified by reading it back) and then writing a 0s pattern (verified by reading it back).
Instruction:
Definition: In this task, summarize the purpose and coverage of the CPU BIT tests.
Emphasis & Caution: Include all tested functional units and stuck-bit checks.
Things to Avoid: Omitting any register or unit mentioned.

High_Requirements: The DPU-1553 CSC shall address hardware modules as defined in document 1400, Company X Specification for the Company X Communication/Memory Module.
Low_Requirements: Checksum LibraryA number of checksum algorithms are required by various system and application software modules.  In order to avoid duplication of code, the various checksum algorithms are encapsulated into a checksum library.
Instruction:
Definition: In this task, explain why and how checksum algorithms are centralized in a library.
Emphasis & Caution: Focus on code reuse across modules.
Things to Avoid: Describing specific algorithms not listed.

High_Requirements: The DPU-DCI shall write to the DCI Memory in B-bit words only.
Low_Requirements: Public FunctionsThe routine formats the given data into a CCSDS Telecommand Packet and writes it to the 1553 bus shared memory for transmission to the SCU.  If a message is already pending in the 1553 shared memory, the pending messages will be replaced in 1553 shared memory with the new message, and an error will be reported.
Instruction:
Definition: In this task, explain how data is formatted into a CCSDS Telecommand Packet and written to the 1553 bus.
Emphasis & Caution: State behavior when a message is already pending.
Things to Avoid: Assuming multiple pending messages are preserved.

High_Requirements: The DPU-SCUI  shall notify the SCU of new data by using the Transfer Request Counter in accordance with interface control document 1143-EI-S19121.
Low_Requirements: Error Collection and ReportingThe DPU-CCM CSC provides a centralized error reporting interface, ccmErrEnq(), that other FSW tasks use to report errors.  Each time it wakes, ccmTask() checks to see if it is time to form an error/event packet for transmission to the ground.  If so, ccmTask() calls ccmHkMkError() to actually create the packet and forward it to DPU-SCUI for transmission to the ground.
Instruction:
Definition: In this task, describe the centralized error reporting flow managed by the DPU-CCM CSC.
Emphasis & Caution: Identify when error/event packets are created and sent.
Things to Avoid: Confusing reporting tasks with transmission tasks.

High_Requirements: The DPU-1553 CSC shall address hardware modules as defined in document 1400, Company X Specification for the Company X Communication/Memory Module.
Low_Requirements: Memory Upload and Download HandlingData can be upload to several types of locations, including* DRAM* EEPROM* hardware registers, and* EEPROM filesystem.ial D_MEM_DAT_UPLD command specify the target location.  If the destination is the EEPROM filesystem, a block number is provided in lieu of a memory address , which is used by the DPU FSW to formulate a filename of the form eefs1:DPU_blk.##, where ## is the block number.  In this case, once the entirety of the uploaded data is received by the DPU FSW, the uploaded data is then written to that file in the EEPROM filesystem.  If a file already exists with that name, it is overwritten.  The EEPROM filesystem can be reinitialized using the command D_MEM_DISK_INIT.
Instruction:
Definition: In this task, summarize how memory upload and download are handled for different target locations.
Emphasis & Caution: Explain EEPROM filesystem behavior and overwrite rules.
Things to Avoid: Mixing memory address uploads with filesystem block uploads.

High_Requirements: The Bootstrap Monitor of the DPU-BOOT CSC shall provide the capability to download configurations of the DPU-RTOS from the RS-232 interface to EEPROM on command.
Low_Requirements: Telemetry Output to the Spacecraft 1553 BusThe SCU, which is a MIL-STD-1553 Bus Controller (BC), periodically reads the STPDU contained in the fifteen telemetry subaddresses, and then writes to the telemetry done subaddress.  When the telemetry done subaddress is written, the scuiTlmDoneISR() is called which gives a semaphore to awaken the scuiTask().  The software provides fault tolerance by automatically awaking after approximately two polling periods.  This timeout is automatically adjusted any time the SCU Poll Rate value is changed by command.
Instruction:
Definition: In this task, explain how telemetry is transferred to the spacecraft via the 1553 bus.
Emphasis & Caution: Note ISR-triggered wakeup and automatic timeout adjustment.
Things to Avoid: Ignoring fault-tolerance behavior.

High_Requirements: The DPU-DCI shall provide the capability for an application program to mask/unmask each of the DCI Interrupt Conditions using the SCM_DCI_CSR.
Low_Requirements: Event ProcessingThe Event Processor Task, after receiving a Mode command from the ICU, configures the DPU DCI hardware, responds to the ICU with a Mode Ready message then receives detector events by calling functions tmaliWait() and tmaliNextEvent().  The Event Processor Task will collect detector events for the time period specified in the Mode command then issue a Mode Complete message to the ICU.  After issuing the Mode Complete message, the Event Processor Task will enter the Idle state.
Instruction:
Definition: In this task, describe the lifecycle of the Event Processor Task from Mode command to Idle state.
Emphasis & Caution: Preserve the correct message sequence with the ICU.
Things to Avoid: Skipping event collection or completion steps.

High_Requirements: The DPU-TMALI shall allocate a ring buffer of size specified by TMALI_BUFFER_SIZE provided during initialization for storing incoming frames of data.  The default buffer size shall be 15 MB.
Low_Requirements: Public FunctionsThis task initializes the TMALI CSC and then continues to read data from the DCI and store it in the TMALI buffer each time the DCI driver signals that data is available in the Ping-Pong buffer.  The availability of data in the DCI Ping-Pong buffer is signaled to the tmaliTask using a semaphore.  Unless a fatal error is detected during initialization this task will never terminate.The specified 3 parameters specify the size of the TMALI queue and the initial parameters for the DCI, if the specified values are outside the allowed range default values will be used.The specified QueueSize is the allocated buffersize (in 4 byte EVENTS), the ring buffer is full when QueueSize-1 EVENTS are stored in the buffer.
Instruction:
Definition: In this task, explain how the TMALI task initializes and continuously transfers DCI data into its queue.
Emphasis & Caution: Note default values, queue size limits, and non-terminating behavior.
Things to Avoid: Misstating when the ring buffer is full.

High_Requirements: The DPU-EDAC shall record the number of MBEs that have been detected in PROM and EEPROM since boot and the address of the last two MBEs.
Low_Requirements: Control and MonitoringEvery time the CCM Control executes, it calls ccmPerProcess() to handle periodic processing responsibilities.  Such responsibilities include analog to digital conversion updates, DPU task monitoring, ICU heartbeat message production, and watchdog strobe. The ccmHealthChk() function, called by ccmPerProcess() verifies the execution of other tasks by monitoring the amount of time that has elapsed since each task last reported.  Other tasks report their execution to the CCM Control Task by calling the function, ccmTaskReport(), providing their task index.  Each task has an expected execution frequency, and if a task does not execute as expected, an error is reported in DPU housekeeping.  If the Command Dispatch Task fails to report for an extended period, the DPU will execute a reboot, since it is impossible to command the DPU if this task is not executing, otherwise it will strobe the watchdog
Instruction:
Definition: In this task, describe how CCM Control performs periodic monitoring and fault handling.
Emphasis & Caution: Distinguish between watchdog strobes and reboot conditions.
Things to Avoid: Overlooking task reporting frequency checks.

High_Requirements: The DPU-BOOT CSC shall complete Stage 1 BIT, and load DPU-RTOS in less than A seconds following a hardware reset if CTS is not asserted.
Low_Requirements: Built-In TestThere are two stages of Built-In Tests (BIT) for the DPU.  The Stage 1 BIT is included in the Bootstrap program and consists of Central Processing Unit (CPU) BIT, and Dynamic Random Access Memory (DRAM) BIT.  The Stage 2 BIT is executed after the operating system is booted and is not part of the Bootstrap program.
Instruction:
Definition: In this task, describe the two stages of DPU Built-In Tests and when they are executed.
Emphasis & Caution: Distinguish clearly between Stage 1 and Stage 2 BIT.
Things to Avoid: Mixing Bootstrap and post-boot responsibilities.

High_Requirements: The DPU-EDAC shall record the number of MBEs that have been detected in DRAM since boot and the address of the last two MBEs.
Low_Requirements: Error Collection and ReportingAt boot time, no error queue exists because it has yet to be created.  Errors that occur in this early stage of error reporting are assigned directly to the global task variable errno.  The reader may refer to the VxWorks Programmers Guide, section 2.3.7 for more information on errno.  If errno is set after the error queues are created, it is queued to the Error Queue by calling ccmErrEnq().
Instruction:
Definition: In this task, explain how errors are handled before and after the error queue is created at boot.
Emphasis & Caution: Note the use of errno before queue creation.
Things to Avoid: Assuming ccmErrEnq() is always available.

High_Requirements: The DPU-SCUI shall be capable of receiving a telecommand from the SCU every N ms and forward it to DPU-CCM for processing.
Low_Requirements: Data ProcessingThe DCX CSC is a task which receives data packages from other tasks, compresses the data using the VBTWL algorithm, and then forwards the compressed data package to the SCUI CSC.
Instruction:
Definition: In this task, summarize how the DCX CSC processes and forwards data.
Emphasis & Caution: Mention the VBTWL compression algorithm.
Things to Avoid: Adding unrelated processing steps.

High_Requirements: The DPU-ICUI shall use DPU-SSI to communicate with the SSI Interface.
Low_Requirements: InitializationThe ICUI CSC is initialized by calling icuiInit().  The icuiInit() function opens the SSI interface port to the ICU, installs the transmit and receive ISRs, and enables Transmit Message Mode in the SSI Driver CSC.  The icuiInit() function is called by the CCM Control Task at bootup.
Instruction:
Definition: In this task, describe how the ICUI CSC is initialized and by which task.
Emphasis & Caution: Include ISR installation and SSI configuration.
Things to Avoid: Misstating the caller or boot timing.

High_Requirements: The DPU-1553 CSC shall address hardware modules as defined in document 1400, Company X Specification for the Company X Communication/Memory Module.
Low_Requirements: Public FunctionsThis routine is used to format messages for transmission to the ICU and to place those messages on the SSI Driver Transmitter Queue. The functionCode parameter is used to create the CCSDS Packet APID and the Message Identifier (refer to document DPUICD-01).  A mutual exclusion semaphore, icuiSemWrite, protects the SSI Driver from being written by multiple tasks simultaneously.  The timeout parameter lets the caller control how long it is willing to block on this semaphore if there is an access conflict.This routine may be called at interrupt context to transmit an ACK/NAK message.  In that case, the semaphore is not taken since it is not valid to take a semaphore at interrupt context.  Refer to discussion of the design to accommodate the ACK/NAK message.
Instruction:
Definition: In this task, explain how messages are formatted and transmitted to the ICU using this routine.
Emphasis & Caution: Cover semaphore usage and interrupt-context behavior.
Things to Avoid: Taking a semaphore in interrupt context.

High_Requirements: The DPU-CCM shall spawn tasks for DPU-CCM, DPU-DPA, DPU-DCX, DPU-SCUI, and DPU-TMALI at bootup.
Low_Requirements: HousekeepingThe TMALI CSC collects housekeeping data that can be retrieved via a call to tmaliHkGet().   TMALI CSC housekeeping is returned via the TMALI_HK data structure.
Instruction:
Definition: In this task, describe how TMALI CSC housekeeping data is collected and retrieved.
Emphasis & Caution: Reference the TMALI_HK data structure.

High_Requirements: The DPU-DCI shall provide an application program with the ability to read and write the hardware ping-pong buffers when in Test Mode.
Low_Requirements: Standard I/O InterfaceThe write primitive does not block.  The buffer passed to write is written directly to the active portion of the ping pong buffer by the driver.  This primitive is valid only in TEST mode.  The write call will set errno to S_dci_MODE_ERROR and return ERROR if the driver is in NORMAL mode.  In TEST mode, the driver returns the number of bytes written.
Instruction:
Definition: In this task, explain the behavior and constraints of the standard write I/O primitive.
Emphasis & Caution: Emphasize TEST mode-only validity and error behavior in NORMAL mode.
Things to Avoid: Claiming the write call blocks.

High_Requirements: The DPU-BIT shall test the Programmable Read Only Memory (PROM) by computing a checksum and comparing it to a checksum stored in PROM.
Low_Requirements: Command and Control CSCThe Command and Control (CCM) CSC is a Level 2 reuse component from the INSTRUMENT Y project.  The Command and Control CSC includes the following components:* A Control Task, ccmCtrlTask(), which initializes the DPU FSW and spawns other tasks at bootup, schedules the production of DPU housekeeping data packets, monitors the execution of other tasks, and schedules the execution of other periodic tasks such as the heartbeat message and the watchdog strobe; and* A Command Dispatch Task, ccmCmdTask(), which receives and dispatches real-time commands received from the SCU or the ICU.Major data structures include:* A Static Data table that keeps track of the operational state.  Data included in this table includes housekeeping production rates, CCM specific flags, and the number of commands executed.* A Command Queue into which commands are placed when they arrive, via interrupt, from the ICU or the SCU.* An Error/Event Queue which accumulates error and event codes which are reported by the DPU FSW.  These error and event codes are removed from the queue and placed into a telemetry packet at a given interval and included in DPU housekeeping data.DPUSDS5.12.1.1.1Flight Software InitializationThe Command and Control CSC is initialized by spawning the CCM Control Task, ccmCtrlTask(), from the operating system startup task, usrRoot().  After the task is spawned it calls a function, ccmInit(), which creates the error/event queue, instantiates needed semaphores and installs various ISRs.  Finally, it spawns the remaining tasks which comprise the DPU FSW. When the CCM Control Task starts up, it reads DPU configuration startup defaults from the SYSTEM_CONFIG_AREA in EEPROM.  If the SYSTEM_CONFIG_AREA checksum is bad, hard-coded defaults are used.  The CCM Control Task initializes the remaining CSCs by calling the applicable initialization function, or if the CSC has an associated task, by spawning the task using the VxWorks function, taskSpawn().  When the CCM Control Task initializes a CSC, it passes the startup defaults read from EEPROM as parameters to the task initialization function. In addition to its task initialization activities, ccmInit(), also initializes the command length verification table by calling ccmCmdLengthInit().  The command processor uses the created table to verify expected command lengths for newly received commands.
Instruction:
Definition: In this task, summarize the structure, tasks, data, and initialization flow of the Command and Control CSC.
Emphasis & Caution: Preserve the correct boot and initialization sequence.
Things to Avoid: Omitting major tasks or data structures.

High_Requirements: The DPU-TIS shall read from the SCM registers in B-bit words only.
Low_Requirements: InitializationThe DPU-BIT should be initialized every time the DPU boots.  The startup task, usrRoot() should call bitPart1() before initializing the device drivers and bitPart2() after initializing EDAC and the device drivers.
Instruction:
Definition: In this task, describe when and how DPU-BIT initialization functions are called during boot.
Emphasis & Caution: Maintain the correct order of bitPart1() and bitPart2().

High_Requirements: The DPU-BOOT CSC shall attempt to complete its normal execution sequence following detection of any failure.
Low_Requirements: Time SynchronizationThe interface with the spacecraft as described in document 1143-EI-S19121 specifies that the spacecraft will provide a Clock Message to each Instrument at a 1 Hz rate.  The Clock Message contains both two values: a value representing the current spacecraft time, and a value representing the delta from the spacecraft time and the Univeral Time (UT), based on an epoch of TBD.
Instruction:
Definition: In this task, explain how spacecraft clock messages support time synchronization.
Emphasis & Caution: Include both time and delta-to-UT values.
Things to Avoid: Introducing unsupported timing sources.

High_Requirements: The Bootstrap Monitor of the DPU-BOOT CSC shall provide the capability to download configurations of the DPU-RTOS from the RS-232 interface to EEPROM on command.
Low_Requirements: Memory Upload and Download HandlingData can be upload to several types of locations, including* DRAM* EEPROM* hardware registers, and* EEPROM filesystem.ial D_MEM_DAT_UPLD command specify the target location.  If the destination is the EEPROM filesystem, a block number is provided in lieu of a memory address , which is used by the DPU FSW to formulate a filename of the form eefs1:DPU_blk.##, where ## is the block number.  In this case, once the entirety of the uploaded data is received by the DPU FSW, the uploaded data is then written to that file in the EEPROM filesystem.  If a file already exists with that name, it is overwritten.  The EEPROM filesystem can be reinitialized using the command D_MEM_DISK_INIT.
Instruction:
Definition: In this task, describe how memory upload and download work for different target locations.
Emphasis & Caution: Explain EEPROM filesystem naming and overwrite behavior.

High_Requirements: The DPU-TMALI shall place the starting address of the event ring buffer in TMALI_HK.
Low_Requirements: Public FunctionsThis routine manually sets the values of the spacecraft time maintained in the DPU hardware, and the UT Delta maintained in a static variable, to the given values.
Instruction:
Definition: In this task, explain how the routine manually sets spacecraft time and UT Delta values.
Emphasis & Caution: Note that UT Delta is stored in a static variable.

High_Requirements: The DPU-SCUI shall use DPU-1553 to communicate with the MIL-STD-1553B Interface.
Low_Requirements: Setting DCI Bit ArrangementThe bit arrangement of the DCI control/status register can be set via tmaliBitArrangementSet().
Instruction:
Definition: In this task, describe how the DCI bit arrangement is configured using tmaliBitArrangementSet().
Emphasis & Caution: -

High_Requirements: The DPU-BIT shall test the Error Detection and Correction (EDAC) on the Company X Communication/Memory Module by reading preprogrammed error locations in PROM.
Low_Requirements: Analog to Digital Converter Driver CSCThe Analog to Digital Converter (ADC) Driver CSC is a new driver which controls and retrieves data from the ADC hardware.  The key elements of the ADC driver include:* A list (array of ADC_LIST_ENTRY) which maintains the setup information and current value (ADC_VALUE) for each of the 24 ADC values. * An API function, adcUpdateNext(), which causes a single ADC_VALUE to be updated every other call to the driver.  A value is updated every other call since each value requires a multiplexer settle time as well as an ADC conversion time.  In order to keep the driver simple, the responsibility for implementing the correct settle time and conversion time rests with the application.* An API function, adcGet(), which returns a copy of the current list of ADC_VALUEs.
Instruction:
Definition: In this task, summarize the purpose, components, and APIs of the ADC Driver CSC.
Emphasis & Caution: Highlight update timing and application responsibility for settle time.
Things to Avoid: Assuming the driver manages timing internally.

High_Requirements: The DPU-CCM shall forward CLK_MSGs to the DPU-TIS for processing immediately upon receipt (I.e. shall not enqueue the message to the command dispatcher queue).
Low_Requirements: Data ProcessingThe DCX CSC is a task which receives data packages from other tasks, compresses the data using the VBTWL algorithm, and then forwards the compressed data package to the SCUI CSC.
Instruction:
Definition: In this task, explain how the DCX CSC compresses and forwards data packages.
Emphasis & Caution: Mention the VBTWL compression algorithm.

High_Requirements: The channel boundary engineering mode shall create an array of look-up table (LUT) channel boundaries, PROD_CHAN_BOUND, from a flat field, and PROD_MN_DATA, using the algorithm described in Appendix C.
Low_Requirements: Ring buffer data structureThe  CSC maintains a FIFO implemented as a ring buffer to temporarily store the data received from the DCI before it is processed. The ring buffer uses two pointers called pIn and pOut to keep track of the data in the buffer. The pointers were defined such that reading from the buffer is the simplest operation and only a single compare is needed to verify if the buffer is empty. The buffer only stores Events so the pointers point to these four byte entities. As the DCI specifies all data in bytes, sizes have to be converted before reading data.
Instruction:
Definition: In this task, describe the ring buffer FIFO structure used to store DCI data.
Emphasis & Caution: Explain pIn/pOut usage and byte-to-event size conversion.
Things to Avoid: Misstating pointer roles or data size.

High_Requirements: The DPU-ICUI shall provide an API for other CSCs to transmit messages to the ICU.
Low_Requirements: InitializationThe SCUI CSC is initialized when the scuiTask() is spawned by the CCM CSC.  Initialization parameters which are passed into the scuiTask() include:* Poll Rate  rate at which the S/C will poll the DPU for telemetry.  The rate is specified in Hz, and has a default of X Hz based on the INSTRUMENT X-DPU poll rate in the Company X 1553 ICD.* Buffer Rate  value used to rate buffer data to the S/C.  The rate is specified in bits per telemetry poll, and has a default of 7664 which is equal to one STPDU per poll (*** bytes per STPDU times V bits/byte).  This default value effectively disables rate buffering.* Size of Low Priority Queue  size in bytes of the low priority telemetry queue.  If the amount requested cannot be allocated, the buffer will be allocated at  the amount of memory contained in the largest free memory block.Size of High Priority Queue  size in bytes of the high priority telemetry queue. If the amount requested cannot be allocated, the buffer will be allocated at  the amount of memory contained in the largest free memory block.
Instruction:
Definition: In this task, describe how the SCUI CSC is initialized and its key parameters.
Emphasis & Caution: Note default values and memory allocation fallback behavior.
Things to Avoid: Ignoring allocation failure handling.

High_Requirements: The DPU-TIS shall provide the capability for an application program to select the active 1PPS signal.
Low_Requirements: Built-In TestThere are two stages of Built-In Tests (BIT) for the DPU.  The Stage 1 BIT is included in the Bootstrap program and consists of Central Processing Unit (CPU) BIT, and Dynamic Random Access Memory (DRAM) BIT.  The Stage 2 BIT is executed after the operating system is booted and is not part of the Bootstrap program.
Instruction:
Definition: In this task, describe the two stages of DPU Built-In Tests and their execution timing.
Emphasis & Caution: Distinguish Bootstrap BIT from post-boot BIT.

High_Requirements: The DPU-ICUI shall provide an API for other CSCs to transmit messages to the ICU.
Low_Requirements: initial boot sequencethe initial boot sequence, identified as Boot Sequence 1.  This processing takes place when the Bootstrap executes from Programmable Read-Only Memory (PROM).  Boot Sequence 1 is composed primarily of 4 steps as follows.First, in this sequence, EEPROM is updated.  Then, CPU BIT and a portion of DRAM BIT is performed.  Next, DRAM is cleared.  Lastly, Boot Sequence 2 is copied to DRAM and executed. During this sequence, the rest of DRAM is tested if skipBit is not set.  Load parameters are put into registers r13, r14, r15 and r16.  DRAM is cleared up to memSize.  Lastly, either the DPU-RTOS or the Bootstrap Monitor is loaded and executed according to the load parameters.
Instruction:
Definition: In this task, summarize the steps performed during the initial boot sequence from PROM.
Emphasis & Caution: Preserve the exact order of boot steps and conditions.
Things to Avoid: Reordering boot sequence operations.

High_Requirements: The DPU-SSI shall write to the SSI registers in 16-bit words only.
Low_Requirements: Hardware InterfacesThe SSI Driver CSC accesses each of the registers on the Company X Communications module using D16 VME access.
Instruction:
Definition: In this task, describe how the SSI Driver CSC accesses hardware registers.
Emphasis & Caution: Specify D16 VME access.

High_Requirements: The DPU-ADC shall provide an application program with the capability to read the current values of the SCM_ADC_MUX and SCM_ADC_DAT registers.
Low_Requirements: Startup SequenceThe DPU FSW is booted using PROM-resident bootstrap software.  The bootstrap software performs a basic set of built-in tests, then copies the DPU FSW from EEPROM to DRAM and executes it.  In flight, there are two methods which can trigger the DPU FSW to boot:* power-on (cold boot), or* watchdog reset (warm boot, commanded reboot).
Instruction:
Definition: In this task, summarize the DPU FSW startup and boot triggers.
Emphasis & Caution: Distinguish cold boot and warm boot.

High_Requirements: The DPU-ICUI shall use DPU-SSI to communicate with the SSI Interface.
Low_Requirements: Synchronous Serial Interface Driver CSCThe Synchronous Serial Interface (SSI) Driver CSC is a new driver which provides an application layer interface to the SSI hardware.  The SSI Driver CSC is implemented on top of the VxWorks tyLib library to manage its ring buffers.   The driver features a standard I/O interface which utilizes an extended ioctl() command set to support unique SSI hardware attributes.  This driver is patterned after the older VxWorks 5.2 serial driver architecture rather than the newer Tornado serial driver architecture due to the lack of programmer control over I/O in the newer Tornado driver architecture.
Instruction:

High_Requirements: The DPU-CCM shall be able to count a consecutively reported error. Low_Requirements: Control and Monitoringthe CCM Control Task also handles memory dump commands.  In the event of a data dump command, the CCM Control Task will break the dump into manageable pieces and dump a small portion at a time, each time the task is awakened.  The purpose of this deferred activity is to prevent a large dump from consuming available CPU time by keeping the high-priority CCM Command Dispatch Task busy for an extended period.
Instruction:

Emphasis & Caution: Emphasize deferred dumping to protect CPU time.

The Built-In Tests CSC, identified DPU-BIT, performs the Stage 2 Built-In Tests (BIT).  The Stage 2 BIT includes a test of the SCM EDAC circuit, a checksum test on SCM PROM, a MIL-STD-1553B internal BIT, and a memory test of the DCI Data Buffers. The results of the BIT are recorded in the SYS_CNFG_AREA in EEPROM and are also maintained in DRAM.  If an error occurs in one of the functions performing the BIT, the test result will be reported as a failure in the test itself.

Unless a fatal error is detected during initialization this task will never terminate.The specified 3 parameters specify the size of the TMALI queue and the initial parameters for the DCI, if the specified values are outside the allowed range default values will be used.The specified QueueSize is the allocated buffersize (in 4 byte EVENTS), the ring buffer is full when QueueSize-1 EVENTS are stored in the buffer.

High_Requirements: The DPU-BOOT CSC shall attempt to complete its normal execution sequence following detection of any failure.
Low_Requirements: Exclude Failed DRAMThe Bootstrap performs a memory test before loading the operating system.  The results of this test are stored in the EEPROM.  The DPU-RTOS uses the results of the memory test to exclude memory segments with bit errors from the system memory pool. The Bootstrap constructs a bit map which effectively implements a list of failed memory blocks during the bootstrap built-in test.  A set of functions have been created to utilize this  bit map to exclude failed memory blocks from the VxWorks system memory pool.  The system memory pool is used by VxWorks to allocate memory dynamically.  In addition, a linked list of good memory segments is created for use by the EDAC Memory Scrubber.  These functions are called from the usrInit() and usrRoot() functions in usrConfig.c.  The dramInit() function must be called prior to kernelInit() in usrInit().  The dramBuildMemPool() function is called just prior to initialization of filesystems and drivers in usrRoot().
Instruction:
Definition: In this task, explain how failed DRAM is detected, recorded, and excluded from system use.
Emphasis & Caution: Preserve required call order of dramInit() and dramBuildMemPool().

High_Requirements: When observing a new GRB, and the XRT_POS becomes available from the ICU, the DPU-DPA shall shift and resize the image and event windows appropriately.
Low_Requirements: Command and Control CSCThe Command and Control (CCM) CSC is a Level 2 reuse component from the INSTRUMENT Y project.  The Command and Control CSC includes the following components:* A Control Task, ccmCtrlTask(), which initializes the DPU FSW and spawns other tasks at bootup, schedules the production of DPU housekeeping data packets, monitors the execution of other tasks, and schedules the execution of other periodic tasks such as the heartbeat message and the watchdog strobe; and* A Command Dispatch Task, ccmCmdTask(), which receives and dispatches real-time commands received from the SCU or the ICU.Major data structures include:* A Static Data table that keeps track of the operational state.  Data included in this table includes housekeeping production rates, CCM specific flags, and the number of commands executed.* A Command Queue into which commands are placed when they arrive, via interrupt, from the ICU or the SCU.* An Error/Event Queue which accumulates error and event codes which are reported by the DPU FSW.  These error and event codes are removed from the queue and placed into a telemetry packet at a given interval and included in DPU housekeeping data.DPUSDS5.12.1.1.1Flight Software InitializationThe Command and Control CSC is initialized by spawning the CCM Control Task, ccmCtrlTask(), from the operating system startup task, usrRoot().  After the task is spawned it calls a function, ccmInit(), which creates the error/event queue, instantiates needed semaphores and installs various ISRs.  Finally, it spawns the remaining tasks which comprise the DPU FSW. When the CCM Control Task starts up, it reads DPU configuration startup defaults from the SYSTEM_CONFIG_AREA in EEPROM.  If the SYSTEM_CONFIG_AREA checksum is bad, hard-coded defaults are used.  The CCM Control Task initializes the remaining CSCs by calling the applicable initialization function, or if the CSC has an associated task, by spawning the task using the VxWorks function, taskSpawn().  When the CCM Control Task initializes a CSC, it passes the startup defaults read from EEPROM as parameters to the task initialization function. In addition to its task initialization activities, ccmInit(), also initializes the command length verification table by calling ccmCmdLengthInit().  The command processor uses the created table to verify expected command lengths for newly received commands.
Instruction:
Definition: In this task, summarize the structure, data, and initialization flow of the Command and Control CSC.
Emphasis & Caution: Maintain the correct boot and task-spawning sequence.
Things to Avoid: Omitting key tasks, queues, or initialization steps.

Low_Requirements: Structural DesignThe TIS CSC provides a library of functions to* initialize the DPU clock hardware at startup,* maintain synchronization of the DPU clock with the Spacecraft clock,* provide a function for use by other applications to retrieve the current values of the DPU clock and the UT Delta, and provide functions for use by the CCM command dispatcher to alter the configuration of the clock interface.

High_Requirements: The DPU-RTOS shall provide the capability to invoke user-installed Interrupt Service Routines (ISRs) in response to internal and external (VME) interrupts.
Low_Requirements: interrupt service routine (ISR)The DPU responds to interrupts via an interrupt service routine (ISR).  VME interrupts are prioritized; however the DPU does not allow nested interrupts, and so any interrupts which occur while servicing another interrupt will pend until the current ISR has returned.
Instruction:
Definition: In this task, explain how the DPU handles interrupts using a non-nested ISR with priority-based VME interrupts.
Emphasis & Caution: Note that interrupts cannot nest and will pend.
Things to Avoid: Describing nested interrupt behavior.

High_Requirements: The DPU-TIS shall provide the capability for an application program to jam the value of the SC_TIME into the hardware, if automatic time synchronization is disabled.
Low_Requirements: DRAM BITPart of the memory is tested in Boot Sequence 1, and part of the memory is tested during Boot Sequence 2.  During Boot Sequence 1, ? KB of DRAM is tested before the Bootstrap is copied to this area.  During Boot Sequence 2, the  remaining memory is tested in ? KB blocks. The DRAM BIT first writes the address of each word in a block to that word in the block.   Then the test flushes the cache and reads back the entire block.  If a single-bit error occurs on the read-back, the location is read again.  If another single-bit error occurs, the test assumes a hard error and marks the whole block bad.  If a multiple-bit error occurs, the whole block is marked bad.  If the value read back does not match the address, the whole block is marked bad.  This same process is repeated with the ones complement of the address. The Bootstrap stores the results of the DRAM BIT in EEPROM as a series of x consecutive y-bit words.  Each bit in a result word represents the result of testing a ? KB block of DRAM.  These bits are mapped to DRAM by proceeding from least-significant bit (LSb) to most-significant bit (MSb) as the addresses of the memory blocks increase.  A set bit indicates that a block of DRAM has failed.  A clear bit indicates that a block of DRAM has passed the test.
Instruction:
Definition: In this task, summarize the DRAM built-in test process across both boot sequences and how results are recorded.
Emphasis & Caution: Clearly distinguish single-bit vs multiple-bit error handling.
Things to Avoid: Omitting failure criteria or result mapping rules.

Instrument Y and Event Mode may be commanded simultaneously in the Mode command. The bootstrap software performs a basic set of built-in tests, then copies the DPU FSW from EEPROM to DRAM and executes it.  In flight, there are two methods which can trigger the DPU FSW to boot:* power-on (cold boot), or* watchdog reset (warm boot, commanded reboot).

High_Requirements: The DPU-BOOT CSC shall periodically strobe the PSM Watchdog Timer at B second intervals (+/- 1/2 second) by writing 0xEB90 to the Watchdog Clear Register, SCM_WDR.  The Bootstrap CSC shall continue the strobe during transition of control from the Bootstrap to the Operating System.
Low_Requirements: CPU BITThe CPU BIT consists of a series of tests designed to test each of the functional units of the processor.  These include test for the Branch-Unit, the Fixed-Point Unit, the Floating-Point Unit, the Interrupt-Unit, and the Timer Unit.   Each of these tests consists of executing a few selected instructions from the this functional unit. The General Purpose Registers (GPR), the Segment Registers (SR), the Counter Register (CTR), and the Condition Register are tested for stuck-bits as part of the Branch-Unit test.  This is done by writing a 1s pattern (verified by reading it back) and then writing a 0s pattern (verified by reading it back).
Instruction:
Definition: In this task, summarize the CPU built-in tests for all processor functional units.
Emphasis & Caution: Include register stuck-bit testing with 1s and 0s patterns.

This primitive is valid only in TEST mode.  The write call will set errno to S_dci_MODE_ERROR and return ERROR if the driver is in NORMAL mode.  In TEST mode, the driver returns the number of bytes written.

Emphasis & Caution: Stress that write is non-blocking and TEST-mode only.
